No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on public resource allocation, fairness, justice, and social care directly supports the foundational aspects of pro-poor policies and social protection systems (Target 1.b, 1.3).
evidence: 公共資源配置的競技場

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture was found in the course syllabus.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare services was found in the course syllabus.

Quality Education.reason: While the course aims to develop analytical skills, it does not explicitly focus on education policy, access to education, or education for sustainable development as defined by SDG 4.

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to gender equality, women's empowerment, or discrimination was found in the course syllabus.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to water resources, sanitation, or hygiene was found in the course syllabus.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency was found in the course syllabus.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's discussion of economic systems and resource allocation has a very indirect and non-central link to decent work or economic growth, making the connection weak.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to industrialization, innovation, or infrastructure development was found in the course syllabus.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly addresses 'public resource allocation,' 'outcomes of interest distribution,' 'fairness and justice,' and 'social care,' which are central to reducing inequalities (Target 10.3, 10.4).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to urban planning, sustainable cities, or community development was found in the course syllabus.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to sustainable consumption, production patterns, or waste management was found in the course syllabus.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to climate change, mitigation, or adaptation was found in the course syllabus.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries was found in the course syllabus.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation was found in the course syllabus.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about 'governance practice,' 'policy planning,' 'decision-making,' and 'public participation,' directly aligning with building effective, accountable, and inclusive institutions (Target 16.6, 16.7).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly covers 'stakeholders,' 'policy networks,' 'social movements,' and 'inter-agency/inter-governmental relations,' which are key components of multi-stakeholder partnerships for policy implementation (Target 17.16, 17.17).
